title: Mailchimp vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp vs Sendinblue email marketing platforms. Pricing, features, and ratings to help you choose the right tool.
intro: Both Mailchimp and Sendinblue (now Brevo) are popular email marketing platforms offering freemium models, but they serve different needs. Mailchimp focuses on user-friendly email campaigns for beginners, while Sendinblue excels at transactional emails and multi-channel marketing. This guide helps you determine which platform fits your business best.

comparison_table.tool_a: Drag-drop editor, landing pages, A/B testing
comparison_table.tool_b: SMS, WhatsApp, transactional API, CRM, chat

choose_a_if: You're a beginner looking for an intuitive, easy-to-use email marketing platform
You want to create professional landing pages alongside email campaigns
You prefer simpler features and lower pricing ($13 entry point)
choose_b_if: You need SMS and WhatsApp marketing in addition to email
You require transactional email capabilities and API access
You want a generous free tier supporting 100,000 subscribers
You need CRM and live chat features integrated into one platform

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Mailchimp excels for beginners and email-focused campaigns with its intuitive drag-and-drop editor, while Sendinblue offers more comprehensive features including SMS, WhatsApp, and CRM tools. Sendinblue has better customer ratings on Trustpilot (4.3 vs 1.5), suggesting superior customer satisfaction.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or Sendinblue?
faq.a: Sendinblue offers better free tier value with 100,000 subscriber limits versus Mailchimp's 500. However, Mailchimp's paid plans start at $13/month compared to Sendinblue's $25/month. For small businesses staying on the free plan, Sendinblue is significantly more cost-effective.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your contact lists and campaign data. Most email marketing platforms support standard CSV or export formats, making migration relatively straightforward. You may need to recreate automation workflows and design templates in your new platform.
